noeuds.auteur: 1. When the government is personal, the ruler is a king.

noeuds.titre: Look up "constitutional debate"2. Monarchs rule for Life.

8. Kingship is divine. Greater on behalf of the Whole, as a person; not like a part.
(Extraordinary, pre-eminent virtue; the best man; living law; supernatural almost)
"Further, the state is by nature clearly prior to the family and to the individual
since the whole is of necessity prior to the part... The proof that the state is
a creation of nature and nrior to the individual I'manarchl ic that
the individual [monarch], when isolated, is not self-sufficing; and
therefore he monarch individuall is ike a nart in relation to the whole
BUT -He- [monarch/individual] who is unable to live in society, or
who has no need because HE Imonarch/individuall is sufficent
for himself, must be either a BEAST or a GOD! A social instinct
is implanted in all men by nature, & vet HE monarch/individual
who first FOUNDED the state was the GREATEST of benefactors!"
"But when a whole family or some INDIVIDUAL, happens to be so
pre-eminent in virtue as to surpass all others, then it is just that
they should be KING of the whole nation. For, as I said before, to
give the authority is not only agreeable to that ground of right which
the FOUNDER of all states... are accustomed to put forward... but
accords with the principle already laid down. For surely it would
NOT be right to kill, or ostracize, or exile such a person, or...
require that HE should take his -turn in being governed--
The WHOLE is naturally superior to the part, and HE who has this
pre-eminence is in the relation of the WHOLE to a part. But if so,
tha only altarnative ic that HE chould hava the cunreme nowar
and that mankind should OBEY him, not in turn, but always!"
"Such an one may truly be deemed a God among men.
Hence we see that leaislation is necessarilv concerned
only with those who are equal in birth and in capacity; and
for men of pre-eminent virtue there is no law--
They are themselves a law! (living law) " (Aristotle in Politics)
